examples: In one type of squirrel monkey, the males lack a visual pigment called L-opsin.
He discovered that each species has completely lost at least one of the seven opsin genes, and some have even lost two.
So the scientists got to wondering: what would happen if they gave a boy squirrel monkey the same opsin that girls have.
When illuminated, these "opsin" proteins change the flow of electrically charged ions within the cell, to help the cell turn light into energy or as a sensory cue.
